channel.title: KiddieDaily — News for Families
channel.description: Daily kid-friendly news with bias indicators and fact checks. Updated every morning.

item.title: London Climate Action Week Foiled by Extreme Heat
item.description: As record-breaking heat crushes Europe, organizers are moving events online to avoid exposing people to dangerously high temperatures. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: French Startup Uses Special Polymers to Better Help Nerves Heal
item.description: The biodegradable material can help improve healing after surgery—or an avocado-related accident. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Even the Internet’s Favorite Pool Guy Doesn’t Know How to Fix the Reflecting Pool
item.description: Algae blooms, peeling paint, and a host of fixes from hydrogen peroxide to nanobubblers have made it hard to diagnose what’s wrong with the Reflecting Pool—let alone how to clean up the mess. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: The ‘Parasite of Parasites’ Has Been Discovered in the Tropical Forests of Borneo
item.description: A newly identified species of fungus attacks the famous “zombie mushrooms” that control ants. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: 3 People Have Gotten Cancer-Detecting Implants in Their Brains
item.description: Coherence Neuro has started testing a brain-computer interface that could one day use electrical stimulation to prevent tumors from growing. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: A Source of Mysterious Repeating Radio Signals From Space Has Been Identified
item.description: Researchers say the discovery could be a “Rosetta stone” for cosmic signals. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: The Most Promising Ebola Vaccine Has Been Sitting on the Shelf for 15 Years
item.description: Years after initial tests, researchers are now racing to see if a vaccine developed in 2011 can help fight the current Bundibugyo outbreak in Congo. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Canada Missed Chances to Inspect Titan Before Fatal Implosion
item.description: A new report shows that government agencies failed to communicate and includes recommendations for stronger oversight in a bid to avert future disasters. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: West Antarctica Is Missing Way Too Much Ice
item.description: Temperatures have climbed up to 45 degrees Fahrenheit above normal, stopping ice from forming in the dead of Antarctic winter. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Why the Longest Match in Wimbledon History Lasted Three Days
item.description: The record will likely never be broken again. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Two Massive Earthquakes Struck Venezuela. Thousands Are Feared Dead
item.description: The country’s interim leader declared a state of emergency on Wednesday following shocks measuring 7.5 magnitude after two quakes hit in less than a minute. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Andy's 'radical rewiring' and 'it's coming homes'
item.description: Andy Burnham's plans to devolve power in Britain and build more council housing lead Tuesday's papers. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Why £15 durians are being sold at half price - or given away for free
item.description: As Malaysian durians are being sold at throwaway prices, farmers worry about their livelihoods. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Sixty-six years on: The promise of DRC independence remains unfulfilled
item.description: The DRC’s independence anniversary renews debate over progress, security and state capacity. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Anxiety turns to joy as Brazil qualifies for the Final 16
item.description: Brazil fans at a fan fest on Rio de Janeiro celebrated after their team overturned Japan’s early lead and won 2-1. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: Why the Reflecting Pool Is Full of Algae After Trump's Renovation
item.description: Warm weather has fueled a bloom that National Park Service workers are trying to kill using everything from hydrogen peroxide to nanobubbles ahead of July 4 celebrations. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: An Alternative to Black Holes: Gravastars with Big Bangs Inside
item.description: Stellar mass black holes may not be black holes at all. Instead, they could be a type of extremely compact star called a gravastar, which mimics a black hole. This is according to theoretical phsyicis — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: World's Most Powerful Collider Shuts Down for a Smashing Upgrade
item.description: Europe's CERN physics research center bids 'Farewell' to the Large Hadron Collider, but it's actually more like 'See You Later, Accelerator!' The new, improved High-Luminosity LHC is due to make its d — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: ESA’s ExoMars Trace Gas Orbiter Has Yet to Detect Methane On Mars
item.description: After more than eight years of searching and with instruments designed to detect it, the European Space Agency’s (ESA) Mars Trace Gas Orbiter has yet to find methane in the red planet’s atmosphere. — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.

item.title: The "Shadow Blaster" Galaxy's Role in High-energy Cosmic Neutrinos
item.description: On September 22, 2021, the IceCube Neutrino Detector in Antarctica caught a blast of neutrinos as it passed through the solar system. These neutrinos were remarkably high-energy and came from a galaxy — 1 outlet covering this story, bias-rated on KiddieDaily.
